recommendations.title: Force Majeure
recommendations.overview: While holidaying in the French Alps, a Swedish family deals with acts of cowardliness as an avalanche breaks out.

recommendations.title: Involuntary
recommendations.overview: In several unrelated stories, the consequences of putting one's foot down – or failing to do so – are explored.

recommendations.title: Triangle of Sadness
recommendations.overview: A celebrity model couple are invited on a luxury cruise for the uber-rich, helmed by an unhinged, alcoholic captain. What first appears Instagrammable ends catastrophically, leaving the survivors stranded on a desert island in a struggle of hierarchy

recommendations.title: Play
recommendations.overview: In central Gothenburg, Sweden, a group of boys, aged 12-14, robbed other children on about 40 occasions between 2006 and 2008. The thieves used an elaborate scheme called the 'little brother number' or 'brother trick', involving advanced role-play an

recommendations.title: The Insider
recommendations.overview: A research chemist comes under personal and professional attack when he decides to appear in a 60 Minutes exposé on Big Tobacco.

recommendations.title: How to Steal a Million
recommendations.overview: A woman must steal a statue from a Paris museum to help conceal her father's art forgeries.

recommendations.title: The Wife
recommendations.overview: A wife questions her life choices as she travels to Stockholm with her husband, where he is slated to receive the Nobel Prize for Literature.

recommendations.title: Boiling Point
recommendations.overview: A head chef balances multiple personal and professional crises at a popular restaurant in London.

recommendations.title: Day for Night
recommendations.overview: A committed filmmaker struggles to complete his latest project while coping with a myriad of crises, personal and professional, among the cast and crew.

recommendations.title: The Edge of Democracy
recommendations.overview: A cautionary tale for these times of democracy in crisis—the personal and political fuse to explore one of the most dramatic periods in Brazilian history. With unprecedented access to Presidents Dilma Rousseff and Lula da Silva, we witness their rise
